ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea beverage' class (FOODON_03301710). No specific code exists for peach-flavoured packaged tea drinks; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0AN covers tea and tea-based beverages broadly. No specific facet for peach-flavoured RTD packet tea; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 represents tea as a substance. No specific concept for peach-flavoured packaged tea drink; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Marigold Peach Tea packet contains approximately 250 ml, equivalent to one single-serve portion as sold in Singapore convenience stores and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Marigold Peach Tea — Nutrition Information Panel (product label); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Peach-flavored tea beverage (FNDDS reference) and HPB Singapore Foodo beverage category data
nutrient_source.organization: Marigold (F&N Foods Pte Ltd) product label; USDA FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Marigold Peach Tea is a commercially produced packaged beverage by F&N Foods Pte Ltd sold in Singapore. Nutrient values are derived from the product's Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as published on the product packaging and brand website, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for peach-flavoured tea beverages and HPB Singapore Foodo beverage category benchmarks. Per-100g values are calculated from the per-250ml serving data on the NIP. The product contains approximately 10g sugar per 100ml (25g per 250ml packet), with negligible protein and fat.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database: sweetened tea beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Marigold Peach Tea (packet). GI of 47 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages and sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured tea drinks, which report GI values in the range 42–55 (low category). The dominant glycemic driver is sucrose/sugar (~9.8g per 100ml). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 25.0) / 100 = 11.75, rounded to 11.8. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~10 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Marigold Peach Tea contains approximately 10 mg sodium per 100 ml, which is low and unlikely to contribute meaningfully to blood pressure elevation; however, individuals with hypertension should monitor total daily sugar intake as high sugar consumption can indirectly affect cardiovascular health.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~9.8 g per 100 ml; ~24.5 g per 250 ml packet)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 250 ml packet of Marigold Peach Tea delivers approximately 24.5 g of added sugar and a glycemic load of approximately 11.8, which can cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption and prefer unsweetened tea alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Marigold Peach Tea contains no fat, saturated fat, or cholesterol, and has low sodium (~10 mg/100 ml), making it low-risk for direct cardiovascular harm; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk, and moderation is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Marigold Peach Tea (Packet) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink peach-flavoured tea beverage manufactured by F&N Foods Pte Ltd under the Marigold brand and widely sold in Singapore. It is available as a single-serve packet of approximately 250 ml, commonly found in hawker centres, food courts, convenience stores, and on food delivery platforms such as field survey. The drink is made from water, sugar, black tea extract, natural and artificial peach flavouring, citric acid, and may contain added vitamin C. It has a light amber colour, a sweet and mildly tangy peach flavour, and a thin, watery texture typical of packaged RTD tea drinks. Nutritionally, each 100 ml provides approximately 40 kcal, derived almost entirely from added sugar (~9.8 g per 100 ml), with negligible protein (0 g) and fat (0 g). Sodium content is low at approximately 10 mg per 100 ml. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low category), but the glycemic load per 250 ml serving is approximately 11.8, which is moderate, making it a beverage that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with caution. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal diets. It is gluten-free and dairy-free. The product is classified as ultra-processed due to the use of flavourings, acidulants, and added sugars. It is not a significant source of vitamins or minerals beyond what may be added by the manufacturer.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from the Marigold Peach Tea product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as published on product packaging, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central peach-flavoured tea beverage data and HPB Singapore Foodo beverage benchmarks. The product NIP is the authoritative source for this commercial product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened tea beverages, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero: this is a sugar-sweetened flavoured tea beverage; protein is naturally zero by product formulation and confirmed on the product NIP. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero: naturally zero by product formulation; no fat-containing ingredients are present; confirmed zero per product NIP and USDA FoodData Central analogous entries. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: no fibre-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per product NIP. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported on the product NIP and not available in the cross-referenced sources for this specific product. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. 8. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as the exact quantity of added vitamin C is not disclosed on the NIP beyond 'contains vitamin C'; a precise per-100g value cannot be confirmed. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg are all -1 as they are not reported in the source data for this product. 10. phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are -1 as not reported in source data. 11. glycemic_index set to 47 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened tea beverages); gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 12. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-sold commercial product. 13. The food is sold by The Noodle Place - Circular Road on field survey data at SGD 1.50.
